fields.name: Document Type
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the type of document that you are about to create.
The sales header forms the basis for all the document types on the Sales & Receivables menu, so the program must label what it is you are setting up, for example, an order or invoice.

fields.name: Sell-to Customer No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the customer who will receive the products and be billed by default.

fields.name: No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the involved entry or record, according to the specified number series.

fields.name: Bill-to Customer No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the customer that you send or sent the invoice or credit memo to.

fields.name: Bill-to Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies the customer to whom you will send the invoice, when different from the customer that you are selling to.

fields.name: Bill-to Name 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies an additional part of the name of the customer that you send or sent the invoice or credit memo to.

fields.name: Bill-to Address
fields.tooltip: Specifies the address of the customer that you will send the invoice to.

fields.name: Bill-to Address 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies an additional part of the billing address.

fields.name: Bill-to City
fields.tooltip: Specifies the city of the billing address.

fields.name: Bill-to Contact
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the contact person at the billing address

fields.name: Your Reference
fields.tooltip: Specifies the customer's reference. The contents will be printed on sales documents.

fields.name: Ship-to Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for another shipment address than the customer's own address, which is entered by default.

fields.name: Ship-to Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name that products on the sales document will be shipped to.

fields.name: Ship-to Name 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies an additional part of the name of the customer at the address that the items are shipped to.

fields.name: Ship-to Address
fields.tooltip: Specifies the address that products on the sales document will be shipped to. By default, the field is filled with the value in the Address field on the customer card or with the value in the Address field in the Ship-to Address window.

fields.name: Ship-to Address 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies an additional part of the shipping address.

fields.name: Ship-to City
fields.tooltip: Specifies the city of the shipping address.

fields.name: Ship-to Contact
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the contact person at the address that products on the sales document will be shipped to.

fields.name: Order Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date the order was created. The order date is also used to determine the prices and discounts on the document.

fields.name: Posting Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date when the sales document was posted.

fields.name: Shipment Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies when items on the document are shipped or were shipped. A shipment date is usually calculated from a requested delivery date plus lead time.

fields.name: Posting Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies additional posting information for the document. After you post the document, the description can add detail to vendor and customer ledger entries.

fields.name: Payment Terms Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a formula that calculates the payment due date, payment discount date, and payment discount amount.

fields.name: Due Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies when the related sales invoice must be paid.

fields.name: Payment Discount %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the payment discount percentage granted if the customer pays on or before the date entered in the Pmt. Discount Date field.

fields.name: Pmt. Discount Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date on which the amount in the entry must be paid for a payment discount to be granted.

fields.name: Shipment Method Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies how items on the sales document are shipped to the customer.

fields.name: Location Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the location where the items are to be received. When you select the customer and the customer has a location assigned, the value is taken from the Customer card. If the customer has no location, but a Responsibility Center is populated, the location code is taken from the Responsibility Center. If neither is specified, the value is taken from Company Information. This field acts as the default location for new lines. You can update the location code for individual lines as needed.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 1 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 1, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 2 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 2, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Customer Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the customer s market type to link business transactions to.

fields.name: Currency Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the currency that is used on the entry.

fields.name: Currency Factor
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Customer Price Group
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code for the customer's price group. To see the customer price group codes in the Customer Price Group table, click the field.
The code is automatically retrieved from the Customer table when you fill in the Bill-to Customer No. field. The field is blank if no price group code has been entered on the customer card.
The code is used when the program calculates the unit price for items on the line. The program checks the Sales Price table to see whether customers in this price group will be charged a different price for the item than the one in the Unit Price field on the item card.
To define the conditions for obtaining the sales price in the Sales Price table, on the customer card, click Related Information, point to Sales, and then click Prices.

fields.name: Prices Including VAT
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the Unit Price and Line Amount fields on document lines should be shown with or without VAT.

fields.name: Invoice Disc. Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code that will be used to find the invoice discount you give the customer.
Microsoft Dynamics NAV automatically retrieves the invoice discount code from the Customer table when you fill in the Bill-to Customer No. field.
The invoice discount is based on the total invoice amount, but it is allocated to the lines relative to the amount on each. On the Actions menu, point to Functions, and then click Calculate Invoice Discount to run the calculation. If there is a check mark in the Calc. Inv. Discount field in the Sales & Receivables Setup window, the amount is calculated automatically. Click here to learn when the automatic calculation will occur.
You define the code in the Invoice Disc. Code field on the customer's card. To define the conditions for the invoice discount in the Cust. Invoice Disc. table, on the Related Information menu, point to Sales, and then click Invoice Discounts on the customer's card.

fields.name: Customer Disc. Group
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the bill-to customer's customer discount group.
The code is automatically retrieved when you fill in the Bill-to Customer No. field. The field is blank if no code has been entered on the customer's card.
When you enter an item on the sales line, the code is used to check whether the bill-to customer should receive a sales line discount on the item. To define the discount, on the customer card, click Related Information, point to Sales, and then click Line Discounts.

fields.name: Language Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the language to be used on printouts for this document.

fields.name: Format Region
fields.tooltip: Specifies the format to be used on printouts for this document.

fields.name: Salesperson Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the sales person who is assigned to the customer.

fields.name: Order Class
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a number which can later be used for statistical purposes.
This field is not used elsewhere in the program.

fields.name: Comment
fields.tooltip:
Specifies whether or not a comment has been entered for this sales header.
You can also enter a comment in Notes FactBox.

fields.name: No. Printed
fields.tooltip:
Specifies how many times a sales header has been printed.
When a quote is changed to an order, the field will be set to 0.

fields.name: On Hold
fields.tooltip:
This field is used when the sales header has been invoiced and the resulting customer entries are included on a finance charge memo or reminder.
You can use the field to determine whether the posted entries will be included in interest calculation.
Before posting, you can fill in the field with your initials or any other code to indicate that the customer entry should wait for approval before interest is added to it. If the field is filled in, the entry is being held for approval.
When you run the Create Finance Charge Memos batch job or the Suggest Fin. Charge Memo Lines batch job, entries that are marked On Hold will not be included in interest calculation and will not appear on the memo.
When you run the Create Reminders batch job or the Suggest Reminder Lines batch job, a reminder will not be created on the basis of an entry that is On Hold. However, if a reminder is created on the basis of another entry, an overdue entry marked On Hold will also be included on the reminder. The On Hold entry will not be included in interest calculation.
After the entry has been posted, the field can be changed in the Cust. Ledger Entry table.

fields.name: Applies-to Doc. Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of the posted document that this document or journal line will be applied to when you post, for example to register payment.

fields.name: Applies-to Doc. No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the posted document that this document or journal line will be applied to when you post, for example to register payment.

fields.name: Bal. Account No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number of a general ledger account or the bank account to place a balancing entry for this invoice (for cash sales, for example). Which account type you can choose, is decided in the field Bal. Account Type.
You can use a search name to enter an account. If you want to enter the cash account, for example, but cannot remember the account number, you can enter the word "cash" and press ENTER. The program will enter the correct account number if you have set up "cash" as the search name for the account.

fields.name: Ship
fields.tooltip: This object supports the Microsoft Dynamics NAV infrastructure and is intended only for internal use.

fields.name: Invoice
fields.tooltip: Uses this field when posting.

fields.name: Print Posted Documents
fields.tooltip: This object supports the Microsoft Dynamics NAV infrastructure and is intended only for internal use.

fields.name: Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the sum of amounts on all the lines in the document. This will include invoice discounts.

fields.name: Amount Including VAT
fields.tooltip: Specifies the sum of amounts, including VAT, on all the lines in the document. This will include invoice discounts.

fields.name: Shipping No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number that will be assigned to the shipment when the sales header is posted.
When you post the sales header, the program will automatically use the next number from the number series in the Shipping No. Series field unless you manually enter a number.
If you manually enter a number, it will not cause a gap in the number series.

fields.name: Posting No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number of the posted invoice or credit memo that will be created if you post the sales header.
When you post the sales header, the program will automatically use the next number from the number series in the Posting No. Series field unless you manually enter a number.
If you manually enter a number, it will not cause a gap in the number series.

fields.name: Last Shipping No.
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally. Tracks the last posted sales shipment number that was used.

fields.name: Last Posting No.
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally. Tracks the last posted invoice or credit number that was used.

fields.name: Prepayment No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number that will be assigned to the posted prepayment invoice for this sales order.
When you post the prepayment invoice, the program will automatically use the next number from the number series in the Posted Prepmt. Invoice Nos. Series field unless you manually enter a number.
If you manually enter a number, it will not cause a gap in the number series.

fields.name: Last Prepayment No.
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally. Tracks the last posted prepayment invoice number that was used for this sales order.

fields.name: Prepmt. Cr. Memo No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number that will be assigned to the posted prepayment credit memo for this sales order.
When you post the prepayment credit memo, the program will automatically use the next number from the number series in the Posted Prepmt. Cr. Memo Nos. field unless you manually enter a number.
If you manually enter a number, it will not cause a gap in the number series.

fields.name: Last Prepmt. Cr. Memo No.
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally. Tracks the last posted prepayment credit memo number that was used for this sales order.

fields.name: VAT Registration No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the VAT registration number for customers.

fields.name: Combine Shipments
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the order will be included when you use the Combine Shipments function.

fields.name: Registration Number
fields.tooltip: Specifies the customer's registration number.

fields.name: Reason Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the reason code, a supplementary source code that enables you to trace the document.

fields.name: Gen. Bus. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the general business posting group that the sales document is linked to. The general business posting group is used to link the sales document to the appropriate general ledger account.

fields.name: EU 3-Party Trade
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the transaction is related to trade with a third party within the EU.

fields.name: Transaction Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of transaction that the document represents, for the purpose of reporting to INTRASTAT.

fields.name: Transport Method
fields.tooltip: Specifies the transport method, for the purpose of reporting to INTRASTAT.

fields.name: VAT Country/Region Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the country or region code for the VAT.

fields.name: Sell-to Customer Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the customer who will receive the products and be billed by default.

fields.name: Sell-to Customer Name 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies an additional part of the name of the customer who will receive the products and be billed by default.

fields.name: Sell-to Address
fields.tooltip: Specifies the customer's address.

fields.name: Sell-to Address 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies an additional part of the customer's address.

fields.name: Sell-to City
fields.tooltip: Specifies the city of the customer's address.

fields.name: Sell-to Contact
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the contact person at the customer.

fields.name: Bill-to Post Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the postal code of the billing address.

fields.name: Bill-to County
fields.tooltip: Specifies the county of the address.

fields.name: Bill-to Country/Region Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the country or region of the address.

fields.name: Sell-to Post Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the postal code of the customer's address.

fields.name: Sell-to County
fields.tooltip: Specifies the county of the address.

fields.name: Sell-to Country/Region Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the country or region of the address.

fields.name: Ship-to Post Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the postal code of the shipping address.

fields.name: Ship-to County
fields.tooltip: Specifies the county of the address.

fields.name: Ship-to Country/Region Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the country/region of the shipping address.

fields.name: Bal. Account Type
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code for the type of balancing account used in this document.
To see the two possible account types, click the field. You can choose G/L Account or Bank Account. The balancing account type determines which accounts can be entered in the Bal. Account No. field.

fields.name: Exit Point
fields.tooltip: Specifies the point of exit through which you ship the items out of your country/region, for reporting to Intrastat.

fields.name: Correction
fields.tooltip: Specifies the entry as a corrective entry. You can use the field if you need to post a corrective entry to a customer account. If you place a check mark in this field when posting a corrective entry, the system will post a negative debit instead of a credit or a negative credit instead of a debit. Correction flag does not affect how inventory reconciled with general ledger.

fields.name: Document Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date when the related document was created.

fields.name: External Document No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies a document number that refers to the customer's or vendor's numbering system.

fields.name: Area
fields.tooltip: Specifies the country or region of origin for the purpose of Intrastat reporting.

fields.name: Transaction Specification
fields.tooltip: Specifies a specification of the document's transaction, for the purpose of reporting to INTRASTAT.

fields.name: Payment Method Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies how to make payment, such as with bank transfer, cash, or check.

fields.name: Shipping Agent Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies which shipping agent is used to transport the items on the sales document to the customer.

fields.name: Package Tracking No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the shipping agent's package number.

fields.name: No. Series
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the number series that was used to assign the sales document's number.

fields.name: Posting No. Series
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code for the number series that will be used to assign a number to the sales document when it is posted.
The program automatically copies the number series code from the relevant field in the Sales & Receivables Setup table:

If the document is an invoice, order or quote, the program copies the number series code from the Posted Invoice Nos. field.


If the document is a credit memo, the program copies the number series code from the Posted Credit Memo Nos. field.

fields.name: Shipping No. Series
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code for the number series that will be used to assign a number to the shipment created from the sales document.
The program automatically copies the number series code from the Posted Shipment Nos. field in the Sales & Receivables Setup table. You can change the code.

fields.name: Tax Area Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the tax area code for the customer.

fields.name: Tax Liable
fields.tooltip: Specifies if this vendor charges you sales tax for purchases.

fields.name: VAT Bus. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the VAT specification of the involved customer or vendor to link transactions made for this record with the appropriate general ledger account according to the VAT posting setup.

fields.name: Reserve
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the reserve option for the document. The option was copied from the Reserve field on the customer card.
The contents of this field, in combination with the contents of the Reserve field on the item card, determine the contents of the Reserve field on sales lines in the document. The reserve option on the item card will be copied to the sales line (thereby overriding the reserve option on the sales header) except in one case: if this field contains the option Always and you enter an item with the option Optional on a line, the sales line will be assigned the option Always.

fields.name: Applies-to ID
fields.tooltip: Specifies the ID of entries that will be applied to when you choose the Apply Entries action.

fields.name: VAT Base Discount %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the percentage to use to discount the VAT element if a payment discount is to be posted. The value is validated against the maximum possible VAT Tolerance % field value in the General Ledger Setup. The maximum value cannot be overridden with the exception of a credit note where you can manually enter the value used for the payment discount.

fields.name: Status
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the record is open, waiting to be approved, invoiced for prepayment, or released to the next stage of processing.

fields.name: Invoice Discount Calculation
fields.tooltip:
Specifies how the discount for an invoice is calculated. You can select from the following options by clicking the field:

Option

Meaning


None

The program will not calculate an invoice discount.


%

The program will calculate the invoice discount as a percentage of the invoice amount.


Amount

The program will calculate the invoice discount by subtracting a specified amount from the invoice amount.

fields.name: Invoice Discount Value
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the value by the invoice discount calculation method you chose in the Invoice Discount Calculation field.
If you chose the option None, the field will be empty.
If you chose %, this field will display the percentage that the program will use to calculate the invoice discount.
If you chose Amount, this field will display the amount that the program will use to calculate the invoice discount.

fields.name: Send IC Document
fields.tooltip: Specifies that an intercompany transaction will be created in the intercompany outbox when you post the document or use the Send IC Document function.

fields.name: IC Status
fields.tooltip:
Specifies whether a copy of the document has been created in the intercompany outbox.

Additional Information

The field can have the following values:


Status


Description


New

There is no copy of the document in the outbox.


Pending

There is a copy of the document in the outbox. It has not been sent yet.


Sent

A copy of the document has been sent from the outbox.

fields.name: Sell-to IC Partner Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the sell-to customer's intercompany partner code if the sell-to customer on the document is an intercompany partner.
If the field contains an intercompany partner code, then the document is an intercompany document. A copy of the document will be sent to the intercompany outbox if the Send IC Document field contains a check mark. Furthermore, when you post the document, the resulting entries will be marked as intercompany entries.

fields.name: Bill-to IC Partner Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the bill-to customer's intercompany partner code if the bill-to customer on the document is an intercompany partner.
If the field contains an intercompany partner code, then the document is an intercompany document. A copy of the document will be sent to the intercompany outbox if the Send IC Document field contains a check mark. Furthermore, when you post the document, the resulting entries will be marked as intercompany entries.

fields.name: IC Direction
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether you are sending the document to an intercompany partner (outgoing) or you received the document from an intercompany partner (incoming) if the document is an intercompany document.

fields.name: Prepayment %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the prepayment percentage to use to calculate the prepayment for sales.

fields.name: Prepayment No. Series
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code for the number series that will be used to assign a number to prepayment invoices posted from this sales order.
The program automatically copies the number series code from the Posted Prepmt. Invoice Nos. Series field in the Sales & Receivables Setup table.

fields.name: Compress Prepayment
fields.tooltip: Specifies that prepayments on the sales order are combined if they have the same general ledger account for prepayments or the same dimensions.

fields.name: Prepayment Due Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies when the prepayment invoice for this sales order is due.

fields.name: Prepmt. Cr. Memo No. Series
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code for the number series that will be used to assign a number to prepayment credit memos posted from this sales order.
The program automatically copies the number series code from the Posted Prepmt. Cr. Memo Nos. field in the Sales & Receivables Setup table.

fields.name: Prepmt. Posting Description
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the posting description to use on the entries that will be created when you post a prepayment invoice for this sales order.
If you leave the field blank, the description on the posted entries will be Prepayment Invoice, Order and the order number that the prepayment invoice is valid for.
The posting description appears on customer and G/L entries.

fields.name: Prepmt. Pmt. Discount Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the last date the customer can pay the prepayment invoice and still receive a payment discount on the prepayment amount.

fields.name: Prepmt. Payment Terms Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code that represents the payment terms for prepayment invoices related to the sales document.

fields.name: Prepmt. Payment Discount %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the payment discount percent granted on the prepayment if the customer pays on or before the date entered in the Prepmt. Pmt. Discount Date field.

fields.name: Quote No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the sales quote that the sales invoice was created from. You can track the number to sales quote documents that you have printed, saved, or emailed.

fields.name: Quote Valid Until Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies how long the quote is valid.

fields.name: Quote Accepted
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the quote was accepted by the customer.

fields.name: Quote Accepted Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies when the quote was accepted by the customer.

fields.name: Job Queue Status
fields.tooltip: Specifies the status of a job queue entry or task that handles the posting of sales credit memos.

fields.name: Job Queue Entry ID
fields.tooltip: Specifies the ID of the job queue entry.

fields.name: Company Bank Account Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the bank account to use for bank information when the document is printed.

fields.name: Incoming Document Entry No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the incoming document that this sales document is created for.

fields.name: Sell-to Phone No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the telephone number of the contact person that the sales document will be sent to.

fields.name: Sell-to E-Mail
fields.tooltip: Specifies the email address of the contact person that the sales document will be sent to.

fields.name: Journal Templ. Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the journal template in which the sales header is to be posted.

fields.name: VAT Reporting Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date used to include entries on VAT reports in a VAT period. This is either the date that the document was created or posted, depending on your setting on the General Ledger Setup page.

fields.name: Rcvd.-from Count./Region Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the country or region from which the items are returned for the purpose of Intrastat reporting.

fields.name: Ship-to Phone No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the telephone number of the company's shipping address.

fields.name: Amt. Ship. Not Inv. (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the sum, in LCY, for items that have been shipped but not yet been invoiced. The amount is calculated as Amount Including VAT x Qty. Shipped Not Invoiced / Quantity.

fields.name: Amt. Ship. Not Inv. (LCY) Base
fields.tooltip: Specifies the sum, in LCY, for items that have been shipped but not yet been invoiced. The amount is calculated as Amount Including VAT x Qty. Shipped Not Invoiced / Quantity.

fields.name: Dimension Set ID
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a reference to a combination of dimension values. The actual values are stored in the Dimension Set Entry table.


Additional Information


The dimension set ID is not edited directly by users. The value of the field is calculated with the dimension values that users select in the Edit Dimension Set Entries window.


The value 0 represents an empty dimension set.

fields.name: Coupled to Dataverse
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the sales order is coupled to an order in Dynamics 365 Sales.

fields.name: Direct Debit Mandate ID
fields.tooltip: Specifies the direct-debit mandate that the customer has signed to allow direct debit collection of payments.

fields.name: Invoice Discount Amount
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the amount that is calculated and shown in the Invoice Discount Amount field at the bottom of sales invoices and posted sales invoices.
Specifies the amount that is deducted from the value shown in the Total Incl. VAT field.
If you have set up invoice discounts for the customer, then the specified percentage value is automatically inserted in the Customer Invoice Discount % field if the criteria are met. The calculated discount amount is inserted in the Invoice Discount Amount field, but you can change it manually. For more information, see How to: Set Up Invoice Discount Terms.

fields.name: No. of Archived Versions
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of archived versions for this document.

fields.name: Doc. No. Occurrence
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number of times the "No." value has been used in the number series.
This number, in combination with the document number, ensures that you always maintain a unique number for the archived document. This is convenient if, for example, you have changed the number series and, as a result, a conflict of numbers arises.

fields.name: Campaign No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the campaign that the document is linked to.

fields.name: Sell-to Contact No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the contact person at the customer.

fields.name: Bill-to Contact No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the contact person at the billing address.

fields.name: Opportunity No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the opportunity that the sales quote is assigned to.

fields.name: Sell-to Customer Templ. Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the template to create a new customer

fields.name: Responsibility Center
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code of the responsibility center, such as a distribution hub, that is associated with the involved user, company, customer, or vendor.

fields.name: Shipping Advice
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the customer accepts partial shipment of orders.

fields.name: Shipped Not Invoiced
fields.tooltip: This object supports the Microsoft Dynamics NAV infrastructure and is intended only for internal use.

fields.name: Completely Shipped
fields.tooltip: Specifies if all the items on the order have been shipped or, in the case of inbound items, completely received.

fields.name: Posting from Whse. Ref.
fields.tooltip: This object supports the Microsoft Dynamics NAV infrastructure and is intended only for internal use.

fields.name: Location Filter
fields.tooltip: Specifies the location filter.

fields.name: Requested Delivery Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date that the customer has asked for the order to be delivered.

fields.name: Promised Delivery Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date that you have promised to deliver the order, as a result of the Order Promising function.

fields.name: Shipping Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies how long it takes from when the items are shipped from the warehouse to when they are delivered.

fields.name: Outbound Whse. Handling Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies a date formula for the time it takes to get items ready to ship from this location. The time element is used in the calculation of the delivery date as follows: Shipment Date + Outbound Warehouse Handling Time = Planned Shipment Date + Shipping Time = Planned Delivery Date.

fields.name: Shipping Agent Service Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies which shipping agent service is used to transport the items on the sales document to the customer.

fields.name: Late Order Shipping
fields.tooltip: Indicates a delay in the shipment of one or more lines, or that the shipment date is either the same as or earlier than the work date.

fields.name: Date Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the filter that filters by date.
If the field contains a date, the values in the fields that contain quantities are based only on entries with the date that is included in the filter.
You can enter a date or a time interval. There are certain rules for how they should be entered:

Meaning
Sample Expression
Entries Included

Equal to

12 15 00

Only those posted on 12 15 00.


Interval

12 15 00..01 15 01
..12 15 00

Those posted on dates between and including 12 15 00 and 01 15 01.
Those posted on 12 15 00 or earlier.


Either/or

12 15 00|12 16 00

Those posted on either 12 15 00 or 12 16 00. If there are entries posted on both days, they will all be displayed.


You can also combine the various format types:

Sample Expression
Entries Included

12 15 00|12 01 00..12 10 00

Entries posted either on 12 15 00 or on dates between and including 12 01 00 and 12 10 00.


..12 14 00|12 30 00..

Entries posted on 12 14 00 or earlier, or entries posted on 12 30 00 or later - that is, all entries except those posted on dates between and including 12 15 00 and 12 29 00.

fields.name: Receive
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally. Specifies whether the document will be posted as a return receipt.

fields.name: Return Receipt No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number that will be assigned to the return receipt when the sales header is posted.
When you post the sales header, unless you manually enter a number, the program will automatically use the next number from the number series in the Return Receipt No. Series field.
Entering the number manually will not cause a gap in the number series.

fields.name: Return Receipt No. Series
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code for the number series that will be used to assign a number to the return receipt created from the sales document.
The program automatically copies the number series code from the Posted Return Receipt Nos. field in the Sales & Receivables Setup table.
You can change the code.

fields.name: Last Return Receipt No.
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally. Tracks the last posted return receipt number that was used.

fields.name: Allow Line Disc.
fields.tooltip: Specifies that a line discount is calculated when the price is offered.

fields.name: Get Shipment Used
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Assigned User ID
fields.tooltip: Specifies the ID of the user who is responsible for the document.
